label: Vector-search and retrieval services

node.label: Vector-search and retrieval services
node.definition: A vector-retrieval service operationalizes embedding generation, indexing, metadata filtering, approximate search, reranking, freshness, and access control.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Vector-search and retrieval services is modeled as depending on Vector databases & ANN indexes, Hybrid retrieval & reranking and feeding downstream nodes such as Neural search, RAG & question answering.
examples: atlas downstream link to Neural search, RAG & question answering
combined with Vector databases & ANN indexes
limitations: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Vector databases & ANN indexes, Hybrid retrieval & reranking.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Vector-search and retrieval services has an `operationalizes` dependency on Vector databases & ANN indexes because Vector databases & ANN indexes is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

In this atlas graph, Vector-search and retrieval services has an `operationalizes` dependency on Hybrid retrieval & reranking because Hybrid retrieval & reranking is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

In this atlas graph, Vector-search and retrieval services has an `operationalizes` dependency on Distributed systems & networking because Distributed systems & networking is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.
